channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: What to know about Panama at the FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Panama are World Cup-bound for a second time in eight years, but in 2026, they're not there to just make up the numbers.

item.title: What to know about Georgia’s primary as Republicans set sights on Senate
item.description: Republicans hope to reclaim Ossoff's Senate seat after 2020 upset; Democrats will pick champion for governor's race.

item.title: Is Christian Zionism in the US on a decline?
item.description: Support for Israel among young American conservatives is weakening. That might not bode well for Christian Zionism.

item.title: Fabio Di Giannantonio wins shortened Catalan MotoGP after Marquez crash
item.description: Race marred by two red flags and multiple crashes, including a serious incident involving Alex Marquez and Pedro Acosta.

item.title: Drone strike sparks fire on perimeter of UAE’s Barakah nuclear power plant
item.description: Authorities say radiation levels remain normal, operations not affected.

item.title: Son of Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas elected to top Fatah body
item.description: Businessman Yasser Abbas, 64, secured a place on the central committee despite spending most of his time in Canada.

item.title: How will Izz al-Din al-Haddad assassination impact Hamas’s Gaza operations?
item.description: Israel's killing of Hamas's top commander Izz al-Din al-Haddad tests the Gaza ceasefire as the group pledges to adapt.

item.title: New wave of Israeli strikes pound Lebanon despite ceasefire
item.description: New wave of Israeli strikes pound Lebanon despite ceasefire

item.title: Pro-Palestine protest held at oldest colonial building in South Africa
item.description: To commemorate the Nakba, protesters in Cape Town staged a demonstration in solidarity with Palestinians.

item.title: Schoolchildren flee Israeli raid in occupied West Bank
item.description: Palestinian children rushed through the streets of Burqa, a village in Nablus, after Israeli forces evacuated schools.

item.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC a global health emergency
item.description: An Ebola outbreak caused by the rare Bundibugyo strain has killed dozens in Democratic Republic of the Congo.

item.title: Israel comes second at Eurovision amid boycotts and backlash
item.description: Bulgaria won Eurovision, pushing Israel into second place amid boycotts and protests over Israel’s war on Gaza.

item.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC, Uganda a global emergency: What to know
item.description: DRC outbreak causes more than 80 deaths and spreads to neighbouring Uganda.

item.title: Republican Senator Cassidy loses Louisiana primary after opposing Trump
item.description: Bill Cassidy is among seven Republican senators who voted to convict Trump after the January 6, 2021, Capitol attack.

item.title: Javier Bardem says condemning the Gaza genocide has led to more work
item.description: Javier Bardem says condemning the Gaza genocide has led to more work

item.title: The strike that changed the geometry of war
item.description: Israel's attacks on Doha and Tehran demonstrate a new capability that may make warfare more unpredictable.

item.title: Two Nepali Sherpa climbers break own records on Mount Everest
item.description: Kami Rita Sherpa sets new record with 32nd summit as Lhakpa Sherpa breaks her own women's record with 11th summit.

item.title: Egyptian teen Amina Orfi beats El-Sherbini to win PSA world squash title
item.description: Orfi, 18, beats compatriot Nour El-Sherbini in a five-game thriller to win the PSA World Championship title in Giza.

item.title: How Arsenal or Man City can clinch Premier League title with two games left
item.description: Arsenal are two points ahead of City but have a slightly inferior goal difference as the season enters its final week.

item.title: Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia kill at least five
item.description: Russian authorities say three people killed in the Moscow region and one in Belgorod in Ukrainian attacks.

item.title: Conor McGregor to fight Max Holloway in UFC return on July 11
item.description: MMA legend will compete for the first time in five years at UFC 329 in the United States.

item.title: Al Jazeera reports from Strait of Hormuz on Iran’s plan to manage shipping
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Tohid Asadi reports from the strait and explains Tehran’s newest plan to manage shipping.

item.title: Bulgaria wins Eurovision for first time; Israel comes second amid boycott
item.description: Spain, Netherlands, Ireland, Iceland and Slovenia stage biggest political boycott in Eurovision history over Gaza war.

item.title: Sardar Azmoun left out as Iran announce World Cup 2026 squad
item.description: Veteran striker Azmoun was not named in the 30-man squad, which will be trimmed to 26 by June 1.

item.title: Federal funding for Trump ballroom in trouble after Senate ruling
item.description: Republicans seek to allocate $1bn in public funds for Secret Service security upgrades linked to the plans.
